title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge
summary: A multi-service application works through localhost on one machine but fails by service name and accidentally exposes its database to every interface. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker network namespace veth bridge increment in least-exposure container network topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress. Competency probe: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries. A defensible solution applies Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-exposure container network topology.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-3
    artifact: network-contract.yaml
    decision: worked docker networking dns publishing 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      frontend-network: published-edge
      backend-network: internal
      service-discovery: service-name
      host-binding: 127.0.0.1:8080
      database-published: false
    verify: docker network inspect intake-backend --format json
    failure: Reject container IP pinning, localhost-as-peer assumptions, accidental all-interface publishing, or an exposed database port.
    repair: Use project-scoped DNS names, separate networks by trust, bind only the required host interface, and test egress and ingress independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A Docker bridge is the same object and trust boundary as a physical Ethernet switch. This fails the Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
